title: lichen_l2_enable(true) after disable fails: link_ctx not re-initialized
description:
lichen_l2_enable(false) calls lichen_link_cleanup() and sets link_ctx_initialized=0 (line 273-274). But lichen_l2_enable(true) only calls lichen_lora_l2_start() - it does NOT re-initialize link_ctx. lichen_link_init() is only called in lichen_l2_iface_init() during interface creation.

Trigger: disable then re-enable the interface (e.g., via net shell 'net iface down 1' then 'net iface up 1').

Bad behavior: After re-enable, both TX and RX paths check link_ctx_initialized and reject all packets with 'TX attempted before link_ctx initialized' or 'RX before link_ctx initialized'. The interface appears up but cannot send or receive.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c lines 228-280
close_reason: Fixed: lichen_l2_enable(true) now re-initializes link_ctx if it was cleaned up by a prior disable. Mirrors lichen_l2_iface_init() initialization sequence.
